prediction.reasoning:
As an AI, I do not have access to real-time market data beyond my last training update, nor do I possess the ability to perform financial analysis, technical charting, or incorporate current news, earnings reports, or macroeconomic factors that influence stock prices. Therefore, this prediction is a statistical approximation and should not be considered financial advice.

The point estimate of $129.50 reflects a very slight upward drift from the current price, a common assumption for equity markets over a short period (20 trading days) in the absence of specific negative catalysts.

The confidence intervals are calculated based on general market volatility principles, using an estimated average daily stock volatility (approximately 1.5-1.6% daily standard deviation of returns). This daily volatility is then scaled by the square root of the number of trading days (20 days) to estimate the 20-day standard deviation of returns. Standard Z-scores for a normal distribution are applied to this volatility to construct the respective confidence intervals around the current price. These intervals represent the range within which the price is statistically expected to fall with the given confidence level, assuming historical volatility patterns persist.
